content.problemStatement:
It is well-known that if you become rich, you will have a lot of trouble. Coach Khaled is one of the richest men in the world. He has NNN jewels (numbered 111 through NNN); for each valid iii, the iii-th jewel has value PiP_iPi​. The values of all jewels are pairwise distinct. Unfortunately for Khaled, one of the best thieves in the world, Ghoda, is trying to steal the jewels.

Khaled knows that Ghoda wants to try to steal jewels during MMM days (numbered 111 through MMM). For each valid iii, on the iii-th day, consider all jewels with numbers between LiL_iLi​ and RiR_iRi​ (inclusive) which Ghoda has not stolen yet. If there is at least one such jewel, Ghoda decides to steal one of these jewels: the most valuable one. Khaled can only choose one day and prevent Ghoda from stealing any jewels on that day; Ghoda can steal jewels on subsequent days in the same way.

Khaled wants to maximise the sum of values of the jewels Ghoda has not stolen during these MMM days. Can you help him?
constraints: P1,P2,…,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1​,P2​,…,PN​ are pairwise distinct
1≤Li,Ri≤N1 \le L_i, R_i \le N1≤Li​,Ri​≤N for each valid iii
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers P1,P2,…,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1​,P2​,…,PN​.
MMM lines follow. For each iii (1≤i≤M1 \le i \le M1≤i≤M), the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers LiL_iLi​ and RiR_iRi​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the maximum possible sum of values of Khaled's remaining jewels.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Khaled can prevent Ghoda from stealing on day 222. Then, Ghoda steals the 333-rd jewel on the 111-st day and the 222-nd jewel on the 333-rd day; he cannot steal anything on the 444-th day. The remaining jewels are the 111-st and 444-th jewel, with values 111 and 333 respectively.
